meta.name: The Crude Oracle $1,000,000 Oil Intelligence Portfolio
meta.description: Educational paper-trading demonstration account. SAMPLE / ILLUSTRATIVE data throughout — all prices indicative, all positions virtual. Update per docs/PORTFOLIO_PLAYBOOK.md.
meta.objective: The objective is to test whether The Crude Oracle's daily intelligence process can outperform standard passive or generic oil-market tools over time through better data, risk control and decision discipline.
meta.disclaimer: This is a virtual paper-trading example for education and platform demonstration only. It is not financial advice, investment advice, trading advice or a recommendation to buy, sell or hold any security, commodity, derivative, ETF, fund or financial product. Trading and investing involve risk, including loss of capital. Past performance and simulated performance do not guarantee future results. Users must conduct their own research and consult a regulated financial adviser where appropriate.

account.currency: USD (non-USD positions converted at indicative FX)

position_limits: High-conviction position: maximum 20% — written justification required
Futures exposure margin-adjusted, notional counted against limits, stop always defined
rules.cash_reserve: Minimum 10% cash unless explicitly justified in writing
rules.risk_per_trade: Target maximum loss per trade 1–2% of account; any trade risking more than 2% carries a special risk warning
conduct: Every trade recorded with thesis and risk before entry

allocation.note: Longer-term oil and gas equity exposure
Capped-risk positions only — under-deployed by choice

positions.target: Re-rate to peer multiples; income while waiting
Income compounding; dividend covered at $60 Brent

positions.asset: ICE Brent Crude Futures (2 lots, 2,000 bbl notional)

positions.target: Range trade with the inventory draw cycle
Expansion FID + new SPAs as catalysts

positions.asset: Gasoil ETC (sample) — diesel crack research theme
positions.target: Crack repricing on outage risk (Toolkit Hypothesis #1, 68%)

positions.stop_loss: £1.10 (-12%, risk 0.30% of account — wide stop justified by illiquidity)
positions.target: UKCS fiscal clarity as re-rating catalyst

closed_trades.reason_closed: Target reached at range top ahead of the weekly EIA print — took the tactical gain rather than hold event risk. Decision label: Exit.
closed_trades.lesson: The process worked: entry on inventory-draw signal (Module 1), exit at pre-defined target. Not holding through binary events is a rule, not a prediction.

trade_log.thesis: Prompt physical market tighter than sentiment: fourth US inventory draw in five weeks, OECD stocks below 5-yr average, backwardation firming — while positioning in WTI (not Brent) is uncrowded. Structure and stocks agree (Curve Dashboard: supported).
trade_log.catalyst: Weekly EIA prints; OPEC+ compliance headlines.
trade_log.supporting_data: Balance Engine implied deficit -0.2 mb/d; Cushing draws; Brent M1/M2 tightening (Module 3).
trade_log.risk: OPEC+ signalling a faster unwind (News-to-Barrels event #2, low confidence but live); crowded Brent length (Module 5, 82nd percentile) means exits are busy.
trade_log.invalidation: Two consecutive US inventory builds, or Brent M1/M2 flipping toward flat.
sources: The Crude Oracle Balance Engine / Curve Dashboard
trade_log.strategy_fit: High-conviction tactical trade (15.5% notional < 20% cap) with written justification: three intelligence pillars aligned (inventory, structure, supply). Justification recorded per rules.

trade_log.thesis: Fleet-supply tightness is structural: ageing fleet, low orderbook, sanctioned-trade inefficiency and floating-storage absorption — freight strength without cargo strength (Flow Map anomaly, fleet-driven).
trade_log.catalyst: New designations shrinking the compliant fleet; seasonal rate strength.
trade_log.supporting_data: TD3C +1.2σ with flat cargo counts (Module 2); Toolkit Hypothesis #3 (55%, monitoring).
trade_log.risk: Rate volatility is extreme; a fast unwind of floating storage releases tonnage.
trade_log.invalidation: Enforcement visibly relaxes; floating storage draws two consecutive weeks; newbuild ordering accelerates.
trade_log.strategy_fit: Services/shipping sleeve; expresses a flow-side insight equities haven't fully priced.

trade_log.thesis: Diesel cracks undervalued relative to refinery outage risk: Rotterdam outage (400 kb/d), distillate stocks below seasonal average, clean freight tightening, crack not yet repriced — against a near-record managed-money short (squeeze fuel).
trade_log.catalyst: ARA distillate draws; Rotterdam restart guidance slipping.
trade_log.supporting_data: Toolkit Hypothesis #1 (confidence 68% — evidence weight, not probability of profit); Module 5 gasoil crowd-risk: elevated.
trade_log.risk: Diesel demand is genuinely weak; a fast restart kills the thesis. Timing risk high — squeezes are brief.
trade_log.invalidation: Refinery returns inside two weeks; distillate stocks build two consecutive weeks; freight normalises.
trade_log.strategy_fit: Speculative sleeve, capped and stop-defined — a hypothesis from Module 6 expressed with controlled risk. This is the platform loop: intelligence → hypothesis → journaled paper trade.
trade_log.special_risk_warning: Speculative instrument referencing a crowded-short squeeze scenario; risk of rapid reversal. Position deliberately capped at 3%.

benchmarks.period: July 2026 to date (3 trading days — far too short to judge; shown for format)

rows.notes: Sample period; cash drag vs pure beta expected early
One closed trade — no statistical meaning yet

benchmarks.note: Benchmarks: cash (SONIA/T-bill proxy), Brent front month, WTI front month, broad energy ETF, S&P 500 energy sector where available. All benchmark values indicative/delayed. Simulated performance does not guarantee future results.

daily_brief.title: The Crude Oracle Daily Trading Brief — 2026-07-04

sections.label: Market summary
sections.text: Crude firm on the inventory draw; both benchmarks backwardated. Physical tightness real at the prompt, suspect in the deferred (Brent 6-month rich vs Atlantic length).

sections.label: Brent / WTI price signal
sections.text: Brent $78.42 (+1.4% w/w), WTI $74.18. Uptrend within range; resistance zone $79.80–80.50 Brent. Brent–WTI $4.24 keeps the US export arb working.

sections.label: Gas / LNG signal
sections.text: TTF eased on storage pace; NBP followed. NGAS-ETC position under water — thesis is winter tightening, stop distance intact.

sections.label: Supply risk
sections.text: OPEC+ unwind signalling is the live bear risk (low confidence). Rotterdam outage removes 400 kb/d of runs. Sanctions designations tightening effective supply.

sections.label: Demand signal
sections.text: US gasoline at 5-yr average; India structural growth intact; European diesel still the weak link — which is exactly why the crack hypothesis is capped at 3%.

sections.label: Inventory signal
sections.text: US commercial -2.1 mb; Cushing below 25 mb toward operational minimums. Draw cycle intact — supports T-001.

sections.label: Geopolitical risk
sections.text: Red Sea reroutings persist; hurricane watch on the US Gulf (two-sided for crude). No position action — monitoring per News-to-Barrels event cards.

sections.label: Top opportunity today
sections.text: Diesel crack dislocation (Hypothesis #1, 68% evidence weight) — already expressed via GSOL-ETC at capped size. No addition; let the thesis work or fail.

sections.label: Top risk today
sections.text: Crowded Brent length (82nd percentile) meeting an OPEC+ unwind headline. Mitigation: futures stop at $75.90, cash at 17.5%.

sections.label: Existing positions review
sections.text: 11 open, all within limits. Tankers best (+3.4%); gas ETC worst (-1.5%), within stop distance. No stops threatened.

sections.label: Proposed paper trades
sections.text: No trade today. Watch only: refiner exposure if cracks reprice; UK gas ETC on storage data. Discipline over activity.

sections.label: Stop-loss / risk levels
sections.text: All stops verified. Aggregate theoretical loss if every stop hit simultaneously: ~4.1% of account — inside the 5% review threshold.

sections.label: Portfolio exposure
sections.text: 82.5% deployed (43% equities, 15.5% futures notional, 14.5% ETC, 2.5% spec equity, 7% other), 17.5% cash.

sections.label: Cash balance
sections.text: $175,400 (17.5%) — above the 10% minimum; held for the hurricane window and OPEC+ event risk.

sections.label: Watchlist changes
sections.text: Added: US independent refiner (crack repricing play, watch only). No removals.

sections.label: Bottom line
sections.text: Positioned with the physical tightness, hedged by cash and stops against the two live event risks. The process, not the P&L, is today's product.

next_events.relevance: Inventory signal — T-001 and P-005 sensitive
IEA vs OPEC demand disagreement tracking
